1.1: . ― At Miletus . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.5: Lois was perhaps the mother of’ Eunice . We know by Acts of the Apostles ,
1.8: . ― Carpus is only mentioned in this passage. ― The coat , was a kind of long, sleeveless coat with a simple opening for the head.
1.12: Tychicus . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.15: Those who are in Asia . In Proconsular Asia. See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.6: I am already being used as a libation. . Among the pagans, libations were poured over the sacrificial victim before the immolation. Now, Saint Paul, who was about to die at the hands of the pagans, may have alluded to this custom. But since among the Hebrews libations were only performed on the victim after it had been sacrificed, the Greek Fathers and most commentators explain this passage as follows: I have been sacrificed; libations have already been poured over me. ; All that remains for me is to be consumed by fire .
1.19: Prisca and Aquila . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.18: The Gnostics did not admit any resurrection other than the spiritual resurrection of the soul when it passes from error to truth.
1.13: THE books The ones Saint Paul is talking about here are probably the Holy Scriptures translated into Greek, and the parchments , the scrolls that contained the Hebrew text written on parchment, like those that can still be seen today among Jewish people. ― Troas . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.17: Their words will wreak havoc., the discourse of those whose conversations are profane and vain. ― Of this number are , etc. Cf.
1.20: Eraste . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.51: . ― To Lystra . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.4: . ― In Corinth. See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.22: . ― Trophime . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.21: Eubulus, Pudens, Claudia . Nothing is known for certain about them. It is assumed that Claudia was the wife of Pudens, and Pudens, according to several scholars, was a Roman senator, the father of Saint Praxedes and Saint Pudentiana, virgins and martyrs. Linus became pope and was the successor of Saint Peter on the See of Rome.

2.1: Therefore, my child, be strengthened in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
2.2: And the teachings that you have received from me in the presence of many witnesses, entrust them to trustworthy men who will be able to teach others.
2.3: Take your share of the suffering like a brave soldier of Christ Jesus.
2.4: In the military profession, no one concerns himself with worldly affairs if he wishes to please the one who enlisted him.
2.5: and the athlete only obtains the crown if he has fought according to the rules.
2.6: The farmer must work first before he can reap the fruits.
2.7: Think about what I tell you, for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.
2.8: Remember that Jesus Christ, descended from David, has risen from the dead, according to the gospel I preach.
2.9: and for which I suffer even to the point of being chained like a criminal, but the word of God is not chained.
2.10: That is why I endure everything for the sake of the elect, that they too may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus, with eternal glory.
2.11: This is a certain saying: if we die with him, we will also live with him.,
2.12: If we persevere in the trial, we will reign with him; if we deny him, he will also deny us.
2.13: If we are unfaithful, he remains faithful because he cannot deny himself.
2.14: This is what you must remind them of, calling on the Lord as your witness to avoid these arguments about words which serve no purpose other than to ruin those who hear them.
2.15: Strive to show yourself in God's service as one approved, a worker who does not need to be ashamed, correctly dispensing the word of truth.
2.16: Avoid profane and vain speeches, for their authors will only sink further into impiety.
2.17: and their words will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
2.18: who have strayed from the truth, saying that the resurrection has already taken place, and who are upsetting the faith of many.
2.19: But God’s solid foundation stands firm with this seal: The Lord knows those who are his, and: Whoever names the name of the Lord, let him turn away from iniquity.
2.20: In a large house, there are not only vessels of gold and silver, but also those of wood and clay, some for honorable uses, others for base uses.
2.21: Therefore, if anyone has kept himself pure from these things, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified, useful to the master of the house, ready for every good work.
2.22: Flee youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love and peace, with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
2.23: Dismiss the silly and pointless questions, you know they lead to arguments.
2.24: But a servant of the Lord must not be argumentative; on the contrary, he must be kind to everyone, know how to teach, and be patient.,
2.25: gently correcting those who oppose them, in the hope that God will grant them conversion to the knowledge of the truth,
2.26: and that, having regained their senses, they will free themselves from the snares of the devil, who holds them captive and enslaves them to his will.

3.1: Know that in the last days, difficult times will come.
3.2: For men will be selfish, greedy, boastful, proud, blasphemous, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, ungodly,
3.3: Without affection, without loyalty, slanderers, intemperate, cruel, enemies of good people,
3.4: traitors, insolent, puffed up with pride, lovers of pleasure rather than of God,
3.5: Having the appearance of piety without the reality of it: distance yourself from this kind of people.
3.6: These are the ones who insinuate themselves into families to captivate young women burdened with sin, consumed by passions of every kind.
3.7: who are always learning without ever being able to attain knowledge of the truth.
3.8: Like Jannes and Jambres who opposed Moses, so these men, corrupted in mind and perverted in faith, oppose the truth.
3.9: But they will make no further progress because their madness will become apparent to everyone, like that of those two men.
3.10: For you, you have followed me in my teaching, in my conduct, in my plans, in my faith, in my patience, in my love, in my steadfastness,
3.11: in my persecutions, in my sufferings, such as befell me in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. The persecutions I endured are countless, yet each time the Lord delivered me from them.
3.12: Likewise, all who wish to live godly lives in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
3.13: As for the wicked and the imposters, they will always go further into evil, leading others astray and being led astray themselves.
3.14: As for you, remain steadfast in what you have learned and of which you are certain, knowing from whom you received it.,
3.15: and that, from your childhood, you have known the Holy Scriptures, which can give you the wisdom that leads to salvation through faith in Jesus Christ.
3.16: All Scripture is divinely inspired and useful for teaching, for rebuking, for correcting, for training in righteousness,
3.17: so that the man of God may be perfect, fit for every good work.

4.1: I adjure you before God and before Christ Jesus, who is to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearing and his kingdom:
4.2: preach the word; be prepared in season and out of season; rebuke, rebuke, exhort, with all patience and careful instruction,
4.3: For the time will come when people will not put up with sound doctrine, but will gather around them a multitude of teachers to follow their own desires and to enjoy what tickles their ears.
4.4: They will close them off from the truth and open them up to fables.
4.5: But you, be circumspect in all things, endure suffering, do the work of a preacher of the Gospel, be wholly devoted to your ministry.
4.6: Because for me, I am already serving as a libation and the moment of my departure is near.
4.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith,
4.8: It is now only for me to receive the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will give me on that day, and not only to me, but to all who have longed for his appearing.
4.9: Try to join me as soon as possible
4.10: for Demas has left me out of love for the present world and has gone to Thessalonica, Crescent has gone to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
4.11: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark and bring him with you, for he is of great help to me in the ministry.
4.13: When you come, bring the coat I left at Troas with Carpus, as well as the books, especially the parchments.
4.14: Alexander, the metalworker, has done me a great deal of harm: the Lord will repay him according to his deeds.
4.15: You too, be on your guard against him, because he strongly opposed our preaching.
4.16: No one assisted me in my first defense, they all abandoned me: let this not be attributed to them.
4.17: However, the Lord stood by me and strengthened me so that the word might be fully proclaimed through me and heard by all nations, and I was delivered from the mouth of the lion.
4.18: The Lord will deliver me from every evil attack and will save me by bringing me into his heavenly kingdom. To him be glory forever and ever, amen.
4.19: Greet Prisca and Aquila and the family of Onesiphorus.
4.20: Erastus stayed in Corinth and I left Trophimus sick in Miletus.
4.21: Hurry and come before winter. Eubulus sends his greetings, as do Pudens, Linus, Claudia and all the brothers.
4.22: May the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you, amen.
